title: Implement Meshtastic node info, owner, status, and config read handlers
description: Why this exists: the app must show a usable local node after pairing. What needs to be done: implement MVP handlers/responses for app requests that populate node identity, owner/user fields, device metadata, firmware/app version, radio/local status, battery/power placeholders, position/time placeholders, channels/config readbacks, and adapter limitations. Map values from LICHEN HAL and local status resources where available. Acceptance criteria: Meshtastic app can read node info/status/config without crashing or indefinite spinners; unsupported or synthetic fields are documented; hardware-unknown fields are explicit placeholders; native_sim tests validate encoded response fields.
notes: Implemented MVP Meshtastic app sync/read handlers for local pairing. want_config_id now emits deterministic bounded FromRadio sync records: nonce 69420 queues my_info, metadata, region_presets, config, module_config, channel, then config_complete; nonce 69421 queues self node_info then config_complete; unknown nonces queue full static + self node sync then config_complete. Added local-info callback so gateway can feed HAL identity/EUI/uptime/capability data, with stable board-agnostic placeholders when unavailable. Synthetic policy in code today: default node 0x4c494348, names LICHEN Node/LICH, firmware 'LICHEN Zephyr compat 0.0.0+unknown', pio_env zephyr, min_app_version 30200, nodedb_count 1, US/LongFast primary channel, telemetry module disabled, no fabricated battery level or position. Follow-ups created for real battery, GNSS/time, peer enumeration, multi-section config records, placeholder policy, and EC2 native_sim bootstrap gap. Review evidence: three independent codereview passes found queue depth too small, test forward declaration, region_presets ordering, shallow codec assertions, fabricated battery metric, and LoRaConfig manual fields with use_preset; fixes were applied. Build/test evidence on EC2 i-08e0af92b1bf7fc52: R1 Neo gateway build with meshtastic_compat.conf passed, FLASH 254048/1048576 B (24.23%), RAM 85720/262144 B (32.70%); qemu_x86 meshtastic_codec passed 13/13; qemu_x86 meshtastic_adapter passed 18/18. native_sim/Twister were blocked by fresh builder host missing gcc/sudo; tracked as project-LICHEN-t2hn.5.2.1.
close_reason: Implemented MVP Meshtastic local app sync/read handlers and recorded remaining non-MVP gaps as child/follow-up Beads. EC2 validation passed qemu_x86 codec 13/13, qemu_x86 adapter 18/18, and R1 Neo gateway build with meshtastic_compat.conf; native_sim/Twister host bootstrap gap is tracked separately as project-LICHEN-t2hn.5.2.1.
